summary: See clues file for full details. 4 tossups, 4 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 4 tossups and 4 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: In 2012, a Paris auction house unveiled a sheet containing fourteen sketches by this artist for a series of quickly executed genre portraits known as this artist's Fantasy Figures.
examples: In 2012, a Paris auction house unveiled a sheet containing fourteen sketches by this artist for a series of quickly executed genre portraits known as this artist's Fantasy Figures.

clues.clue: In a painting by this artist, a child raises a stick with a rock hanging from it to the hand of a woman whose cheek is being tickled by a piece of straw.
examples: In a painting by this artist, a child raises a stick with a rock hanging from it to the hand of a woman whose cheek is being tickled by a piece of straw.

clues.clue: That painting is a metaphor for courtship and is a companion to this artist's erotically charged depiction of a girl on a (*) see-saw.
examples: That painting is a metaphor for courtship and is a companion to this artist's erotically charged depiction of a girl on a (*) see-saw.

clues.clue: This artist painted a layman instead of a bishop for a painting whose left side contains a depiction of Falconet's Menacing Love.
examples: This artist painted a layman instead of a bishop for a painting whose left side contains a depiction of Falconet's Menacing Love.

clues.clue: This artist of Blind Man's Bluff and the "Progress of Love" series, painted a slipper flying off the foot of a woman as a reclining man looks up her pink dress.
examples: This artist of Blind Man's Bluff and the "Progress of Love" series, painted a slipper flying off the foot of a woman as a reclining man looks up her pink dress.

clues.clue: Pierre Rosenberg argued that an artist with this surname, not her husband, painted miniatures including a boy in pale clothing with a black-feathered hat.
examples: Pierre Rosenberg argued that an artist with this surname, not her husband, painted miniatures including a boy in pale clothing with a black-feathered hat.

clues.clue: Similar to Houdon , an anatomist with this surname created a flayed horse and rider for a veterinary school in Maisons-Alfort .
examples: Similar to Houdon , an anatomist with this surname created a flayed horse and rider for a veterinary school in Maisons-Alfort .

clues.clue: An artist with this surname painted a series of subjects in vaguely Spanish clothing for his portraits de fantaisie, which include a woman in a white-collared yellow dress with an open book, Young Girl Reading.
examples: An artist with this surname painted a series of subjects in vaguely Spanish clothing for his portraits de fantaisie, which include a woman in a white-collared yellow dress with an open book, Young Girl Reading.

clues.clue: The Frick Collection houses a series by an artist with this surname on "four ages" of courtship.
examples: The Frick Collection houses a series by an artist with this surname on "four ages" of courtship.

clues.clue: Madame du Barry commissioned The Progress of Love from that artist, who also painted a Cupid holding a finger to its lips as a man in the bushes looks up a woman's dress.
examples: Madame du Barry commissioned The Progress of Love from that artist, who also painted a Cupid holding a finger to its lips as a man in the bushes looks up a woman's dress.

clues.clue: In one work by this man, a man reaches for the title object and is restrained by his lover.
examples: In one work by this man, a man reaches for the title object and is restrained by his lover.

clues.clue: In that painting, the artist illustrates the couple's profane love by his use of chiaroscuro and by the depiction of a red curtain in the left of the scene.
examples: In that painting, the artist illustrates the couple's profane love by his use of chiaroscuro and by the depiction of a red curtain in the left of the scene.
